ThingDef.label: orthodox path — martial scripture
ThingDef.description:
Written by an unnamed war-cultivator of the Sword Veil Sect, this text describes a cultivation method that channels qi as a cutting, precise force. Every breath is a blade. Every heartbeat, a decisive strike.
Those who walk this path do not simply cultivate — they sharpen. Their qi flows not as a river but as an edge: clean, directed, unstoppable.
Your strikes will carry the weight of heaven's will. Your body will not know doubt.
The choice, once made, cannot be undone.
Requires an awakened Spiritual Root.

ThingDef.label: orthodox path — sage scripture
ThingDef.description:
Attributed to a hermit who spent forty years meditating beneath a jade waterfall, this text teaches cultivation through absolute stillness. The mind is a lake. Disturbances pass over it but do not disturb the depths.
Those who master this method cultivate at a measured pace — but their foundation is unshakeable. Their clarity of mind borders on divine. They do not break.
The choice, once made, cannot be undone.
Requires an awakened Spiritual Root.

ThingDef.label: demonic path — merciless scripture
ThingDef.description:
A bloodstained text of unknown origin, written in a hand that grew steadier as the pages continued — as if the author's body was hardening as they wrote. The method described refines the body through sustained pain and aggression.
The flesh becomes stone. The blood becomes oil. A Merciless Demon does not die easily.
The choice, once made, cannot be undone.
Requires an awakened Spiritual Root.

ThingDef.label: demonic path — abyssal scripture
ThingDef.description:
A scroll that hums faintly when held. Its words describe the void as an infinite appetite — the cultivator does not gather qi, they become a hunger that draws qi in from everything around them.
The power is staggering. The cost to sanity is real.
The choice, once made, cannot be undone.
Requires an awakened Spiritual Root.
